items.snippet: Petroleum markets in the second quarter of 2026 (2Q26) were characterized by continued disruptions to international crude oil and petroleum product flows through the Strait of Hormuz, contributing to higher and more volatile crude oil prices through most of the quarter. The disruptions also resulted in international buyers seeking alternative supply sources for petroleum products, driving up U.S. refinery margins, production, and exports.

items.snippet: Horizon Gold Ltd managing director Scott Williamson talked with Proactive about the definitive feasibility study for the company’s 100%-owned Gum Creek Gold Project in Western Australia and its planned transition from explorer to developer. The open-pit study outlines a financially robust development pathway targeting first production in the second half of 2028. items.snippet: Enthusiasm for tech shares boosted Asian stocks for a second day Wednesday, as more mayhem in the Middle East lifted Brent crude back above $92 a barrel and the yen came under renewed pressure.Tech stocks have had a bumpy ride because of fears that the buzz around artificial intelligence may not match reality and that shares in the sector are overcooked.
items.title: Tech lifts Asia stocks, Brent crude back above $92

items.snippet: Taiwan's Wistron, a supplier to Nvidia, launched a $700 million ‌manufacturing facility in Texas on Tuesday to ‌produce the U.S. chipmaker's latest AI systems, as Taiwanese electronics ​makers expand U.S. production to meet soaring demand for AI infrastructure. • The Fort Worth facility manufactures Nvidia's GB300 Grace Blackwell ‌Ultra Superchip. Nvidia ⁠CEO Jensen Huang has described the AI system built around the product as "the ⁠most powerful AI supercomputer in the world."
items.title: Nvidia supplier Wistron launches $700 million Texas factory for AI system production
